title: Competitor dossier: starter discovery trace demo
competitive_frame: Tools and templates a new team might compare against when deciding whether ProductOS offers a more useful operating model than a generic starter.
comparison_basis: Compared on whether they show a readable lifecycle story, not just whether they cover planning or execution tasks broadly.
research_scope: Lightweight starter comparison for teams learning the ProductOS lifecycle trace model.
credible_wedge_for_posture: Win as a niche starter that teaches a strong operating model instead of shipping a blank scaffold.
required_proof_to_displace: New teams understand ProductOS faster with the seeded trace than with a blank starter
recommended_positioning_angle: Position the starter as a traceable operating model, not another template bundle.

competitors.name: Jira Product Discovery
competitors.target_customer: Teams wanting connected discovery and delivery planning.
competitors.positioning_summary: Provides discovery capture, prioritization, and roadmap linkage to delivery.
competitors.go_to_market_motion: Expansion from the broader Atlassian ecosystem.
competitors.pricing_signal: Subscription SaaS with broader suite leverage.
competitors.positioning_gap: Stronger connected workflow story than a blank starter, but weaker as a one-file demonstration of full lifecycle traceability.
weaknesses: Lifecycle explanation can still span multiple surfaces
where_they_win: Teams prioritizing ecosystem continuity over operating-model clarity
where_they_lose: Teams wanting one inspectable lifecycle example before customization
implications: The starter trace model should make the full discovery story easier to review in one place.

competitors.name: Linear
competitors.positioning_summary: Unified product development workflow from roadmap to release.
competitors.positioning_gap: Stronger execution coherence, but not a focused teaching surface for lifecycle trace discipline.
weaknesses: PM audit lineage is not the main surface
where_they_win: Teams optimizing for execution speed and coherence
where_they_lose: Teams wanting a concrete ProductOS-style trace model before tool rollout
displacement_barriers: Linear's execution ergonomics and product-led familiarity
implications: The starter should show why traceability matters before teams optimize for speed.
